term: paṭhaviṃ paṭhavito abhiññāya paṭhaviṃ mā maññi
preferred_translation: having directly known earth as earth, one should not take oneself to be earth
alternative_translations: having directly known earth as earth, one is not to take oneself to be earth
discouraged_translations: having understood earth, one should not think earth
definition: MN 1 prohibitive formula surface pairing direct knowing with the instruction not to slide into selfing.
notes: Use this record for the trainee-side control line in MN 1. The verb pair matters: abhijānāti marks direct knowing, while mā maññi blocks the ordinary-person movement into selfing and appropriation.

example_phrases.pali: paṭhaviṃ paṭhavito abhiññāya paṭhaviṃ mā maññi
example_phrases.translation: having directly known earth as earth, one should not take oneself to be earth
example_phrases.notes: Control line for the prohibitive trainee-side pattern.

authority_basis.scope: Supports the trainee-side line where direct knowing blocks the slide into selfing.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use this as the standard English control line for the trainee-side MN 1 pattern.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not weaken the line into generic understanding or thought language, and do not lose the prohibitive force of mā maññi.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents the trainee-side pattern from losing either the direct-knowing verb or the explicit prohibition on selfing.
